title: sorbus aria subsp. cretica
intro: Sorbus aria subsp. cretica, also known as the Cretan whitebeam, is a subspecies of the whitebeam tree native to the island of Crete in Greece. This deciduous shrub or small tree is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The leaves and bark of Sorbus aria subsp. cretica have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin infections, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and antioxidant protection. Herbal preparations of Sorbus aria subsp. cretica can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
